dialog: This is Harvey Milk speaking on Friday November 18th. This is to be played only in the event of my death by assassination...
During one of the early campaigns, I started opening my speeches with the same line and it sort of became my signature...
Hello, I'm Harvey Milk, and I'm here to recruit you.
If I was speaking to a slightly hostile or mostly straight audience, I'd try to break the tension with a joke...
I know, I know, I'm not what you were expecting, but I left my high heels at home.

I fully realize that a person who stands for what I stand for - an activist, a gay activist - becomes the target or potential target for a person who is insecure, terrified, afraid or very disturbed themselves...
As President of the Board of Supervisors, it's my duty to make this announcement. Both Mayor Moscone and Supervisor Harvey Milk have been shot... and killed.
Police are looking for a suspect... Supervisor Dan White.
Something has happened.

Knowing that I could be assassinated at any moment, I feel it's important that some people should understand my thoughts.  I think that-- I wish I had time to explain everything I did. Almost everything that was done was done with an eye on the gay movement.
Hey.  I'm Harvey.
Okay, Harvey...
Today's my birthday. No, today is actually my birthday. Well, tonight.  At midnight.
Really?
And, believe it or not, I don't have any plans.  People took me out after work...
Work?  So, that would be...  Let me guess.  Ma Bell or AT&T.
The Great American Insurance Company.  I'm part of the big, evil, corporate establishment that, let me guess, you think is the cause of every evil thing in the world from Vietnam to diaper rash.
You left out bad breath.
So...  You're not going to let me celebrate my birthday all by myself are you?
Listen, Harvey, you're kind of cute for a suit...  But I don't do guys over forty.
Well then, I'm in luck.
How's that?
Because I'm still thirty-nine... And it's only eleven-fifteen.
Where are you from?
Jackson, Mississippi.
Well, this isn't Jackson, but you still can't respond to every strange man you meet on a subway platform. It's too dangerous.
Now you tell me.
There's nothing tougher than a New York City cop. They're arrogant, and they're everywhere. I'll show you the cruising spots, if you want, if you promise you'll be careful... little Scottie-san.
Are you on uppers or what?
No. This is just... plain old me.
You're afraid of the cops?
I'm just discreet. I know a lot of people here. If they found out, I could lose my job.
Oh. You're one of "those." I think you need a change of scenery. New friends.
I need a change.
You're forty now.
Forty years old and I haven't done a thing I'm proud of.
You keep eating like this and you'll be a fat ass by fifty.
No. Don't you worry. I won't make it to fifty.
Why don't we run away together?
Where to?
In those days, San Francisco was the place where everyone wanted to go...  To drop out... To fall in love...
But by 1972, The Haight was boarded up. Crime-filled. Drug-filled. The new place for us refugees was this run down Irish Catholic neighborhood in Eureka Valley. Six blocks square... The Castro.
I cashed my last unemployment check yesterday...
Well, I hope you did something useful with it.
I bought an ounce of pot.  I was just wondering if you had any plans, or thoughts... About us doing something to, you know, bring in an income.
Don't move.
I suppose I can wait tables.
No way.  I want you right here with me... Did you see place downstairs is for rent?
Yeah.
I was thinking we could open a shop together.
What kind of shop?
One with very little overhead. Not too much work... A little one, just like Morris and Minnie Milk of Woodmere, New York...
Just a little to the left...
Hello there.  I'm Harvey Milk.
You're the new renters?
Yes. Welcome to Castro Camera.
I want to join the, um...  What is it?  The Eureka Valley Merchant's Association.  I want to help in any way possible.  I'm no interloper. A Jew perhaps, but I hope you'll forgive that.
I don't think your application will be approved, Mr. Milk. This is a family neighborhood. Your kind are far more welcome on Haight street.
What "kind" do you mean, sir?
The Merchant's Association will have the police pull your license if you open your doors.
Based on what law?
There's man's law and there's God's law in this neighborhood and in this City.  The San Francisco Police force is happy to enforce either.
Thanks for the warm welcome to the neighborhood!
We pay taxes you know!

We'll form our own business association!  Start with the gay- owned businesses... We'll take down the addresses of every customer that comes in for a roll of film. We'll ask them what they want to see done here... what they want changed... We'll get money rolling into this neighborhood. Revitalize it...
Can I come in now?
One more minute! I'll go to the neighborhood banks. They must have some gay customers.
Harvey.  What's with all this political activist crap?  I thought you were a goddamn Republican.
I'm a businessman, Scott.  I think businesses ought to treat their customers right.  Even their gay customers. For God's sake, this is San Francisco!
Harvey.  It's just like every other city in this country.  People hate us. Big surprise.
We should have at least one block in one city right? Start there and take over the neighborhood. Okay, you can come in now!
Wow.  This is--
Happy birthday!
Thanks for joining the Castro Village Association!
Excuse me gentleman... ladies. Hello! Mr. McConnelly!
I just wanted to stop in and see how business was doing.
Fine.
So you don't mind all these homosexuals in here, do you?
No. No. Just a joke. Mr. McConnelly here loves our kind. Spend away! Tell your wife Hello for me.
And people started hanging around our store. Not customers. Activists, kids, folks who needed a home away from home...

There was Danny Niccoletta, a cute art student who I'd picked up at Toad Hall. Turns out he was also good at running the Camera Shop... something Scott and I found we had surprising little interest in...
Opera is passe, Harvey. It's about street theater, now.
And somewhere along the line Jim picked up a protege, a cute political kid from Wisconsin... Dick Pabich... And of course there was Dennis Peron, who ran a very popular business with the new kids...
And on occasion we actually had a real customer. But only occasionally.

What you're not getting is the spectacle of it, the bigger than life emotions… Jim Rivaldo. A great mind. A great wit. A Harvard graduate, which nobody cared about, in those days, in the Castro... Including himself.
Harvey... This guy wants to talk to you.
Welcome. What can we do for you?
Hi, I'm Allan Baird. Teamsters representative, and Director of the Coors beer boycott in California.
Everyone, this is Allan Baird. He's a very big deal, so behave yourselves.
I was told you were like the Mayor of the Castro, said you organized the CVA, you might know some people in the bars.
I do have friends in awfully low places, but just so you know, it's not official yet.
What's that?

The Mayor part. I've considered gathering an army of queers to march up to City Hall and secede, but so far the young gays seem more interested in jeeps and jeans.  But if I could be of any use, I'm all ears!
We can't get Coors to go union. These guys are like me, they can't be out of work too long.
I'm just curious. Are we your first stop or your last?
It's been a tough one, Mr. Milk.
Well, you seem to have noticed that my kind of people drink a lot of beer. If we're going to fast for your sake, I think we should get to deliver some of it when you win this fight, don't you?
Is that what you really want?
And allies. I'll have Coors out of every bar in the Castro by the end of the week. Deal?
Thank you Mr. Milk.
No more Coors beer. It's gotta come out of every bar. Gay and straight.
What's he giving you?
Nothing for me. That's a convert.
A convert to what?
The cause.

I got "my people" to get Coors out of all the Gay Bars. 30 million bottles worth in San Francisco alone. Coors beer immediately fell from number 1. They caved. One week later, union man Allan Baird hired the first ever openly gay union boys to drive Teamsters trucks... We weren't just a group of pansies anymore. We had a neighborhood. We had the unions. And for the first time, we had a little bit of power.
Maybe that was the day someone first called me the "Mayor" of Castro Street. Or perhaps I invented it myself.
The fucking cops, man, they're up on Castro Street! Toad Hall.
Clear the sidewalk!
...if we had someone in the government who saw things the way we see them, the way the black community has black leaders who look out for their interests--
You're gonna run for Supervisor, is that the idea?
I could go right for mayor, but I think I should work my way up to it... You'll be my campaign manager.
Because I have so much experience in politics.
Politics is theater. It doesn't matter if you win. You make a statement.  You say, "I'm here, pay attention to me."
Harvey, you do that every time you cross the street.
Coors looks like it might cave. The union boys will owe me. Imagine if we get their vote... It'll be fun!
Fun?
We'll make it a spectacle.
A week ago, Police officers came into our area with covered up badges and sent fourteen of our people to the hospitals, and to jail. The charges, "Blocking a Sidewalk."
--Let us have our tax money go for our protection and not our persecution. Worry about gun control and not marijuana control.
School supplies, seniors, not what books I read--
My fellow degenerates, I would like to announce my candidacy for San Francisco City Supervisor!
Public dental care for retired people... Legalize pot, vote Milk.
Hey, I like the way your pants fit... Where are you from, kid?
Sorry old man, not interested.
Where's home?
Phoenix.
I'm Harvey Milk. I'm running for Supervisor. What's your name?
Cleve... Jones.
Well Mr. Jones, we should walk up to my camera shop and register you.
Fuck that. Elections of any kind are a bourgeois affectation.
Is that right? Do you trick up on Polk Street?
If I need the cash... But I'm selective about my clients.
Tell me one thing before you get back to work then. What was it like to be a little queer in Phoenix?
I faked a lung disease to get out of PE. So what? What are you, some kind of street shrink?
Sometimes. But what I'm saying is, Phoenix can change. But we have to start with our street: rent control, police abuse, pot, parks, seniors.
Well, good luck with all that.
You know what I think, Cleve Jones?
That you're gonna get somewhere if you keep talking?
No. That you need to do what you're good at. Be a prick. Fight City Hall. Fight the cops. Fight the people who made you come here to do what you do.
Sorry old man, I'm leaving for Spain tomorrow. Europe. Got all the cash I need in my back pocket.
I didn't come out until very late in life. There was nobody to--
--look up to, no hope for a better life. Those that did were outcast... arrested.. suicide. And the young ones, who did escape home... and who arrive in San Francisco, and then what...
The “fruit” was walking home with his “trick” when they were jumped.
Name's Robert Hillsborough. Did you know him?
He used to come into my shop.  Are there any witnesses?
Just the "trick." Jerry Taylor.
Jerry wasn't a trick. They were lovers.
Call it what you will. He's our only witness and he says he can't identify the attackers.
There'd be a dozen witnesses if they thought you boys had any real interest in keeping them safe.
You’re the Mayor of Castro Street now, right Harvey? You really wanna help? Get your people to show some discretion on the streets, huh?
Well, it looks like the big guns and the rich gays in San Francisco are going to back Dianne Feinstein and other straight candidates they consider "gay-friendly."
Who cares about those old queens?
Why isn't my campaign manager leading the meeting?
I'm beat, Harvey.  I passed out flyers for seven hours straight.
In the bathhouse?
People in bathhouses vote.  I'm tired. I'm taking a nap.
Just checking the exposure. That a new boyfriend?  He's very cute.
Um...  Thanks, Harvey.
So who are these so-called "gay leaders?"  And who appointed them? And what are you suggesting, Jim, that I'm supposed to go and solicit their endorsement?
Harvey--
We're down here, on the streets, trying to help people, so where they hell are they?
David Goodstein is the publisher of The Advocate and he has a house--
A mansion--
--in Atherton.
Harvey, you should read this.
"Harvey Milk will have a dream journey and nightmare to hell, a night of horror. You will be stabbed and have your genitals, cock, balls, prick cut off."
They probably wrote it. Hey, If somebody tries to kill me, I’ll get the sympathy vote, right? Could be just the push we need.
You think this is funny? Look at it.
It's a complete joke. No rhythm. No humor. It's insulting.
Don't do that.
If you put it away, hide it in a drawer, it'll just get bigger and scarier.  Now it's there.  We'll see it every day.  It can't get us.
All this energy... to make yourself a target.  And what for?  Something we have no chance to win.
I keep telling you, it's not just about winning.

The "top gays" in San Francisco were David Goodstein and his lawyer sidekick Rick Stokes. David was a rich old queen who'd bought the biggest gay magazine, The Advocate. He had it delivered to subscribers in brown paper bags so no-one would know... That's how he lived his life, covered up, discreet.

I worked for a financial institution.  I was very discreet. But someone spotted me at the opera, sitting in a box, next to my lover.  And I was fired.  That's when I decided to do something.  So I bought The Advocate.  And I use my money and my influence in quiet ways, to do what I can...
Endorsing straight candidates is what you think is best to help us?
Yes, if they're friendly to our cause.
Politics is complicated in San Francisco, and Supervisor is a citywide office. Political alliances need to be built. You can't simply move here from across the country and run for office.
But I am running for office. I'm on the ballot. I have the union rank and file, the seniors, and I could use your magazine's endorsement.
Harvey, we're like the Catholic church. We welcome converts, but we don't make them Pope the same day.
And why haven't you run?
It's too early for a gay candidate for Supervisor.  Especially one from the Castro. It's bad for the community's image.
What's wrong with the Castro?
Nobody works there.  It's all sex and drugs and more sex and no one taking responsibility for their own problems.
The cops come into our neighborhood and beat us.  They turn a blind eye if one of us is murdered.
Then we file a lawsuit against the city. We go to our political allies in City Hall...
There are no real gay allies. Yes, our friends may sense our struggle, but they can't feel it. It's not life or death for them. We need our own in office.
You can't demand acceptance overnight.
Why not? How can we ask them to respect us if we don't show ourselves some self respect, David.
The more “out” you make us, the more you incite them. Step back and quiet down.

Go back into the closet?  Is that what you're saying?  Because I know the closet. I lived in it for more years than I care to remember. I don't need your endorsement. I'm not asking anyone for acceptance. I don't have time to ask.
For you, politics is a game, a lark.  Like putting on a rock festival or staging a "love-in." You're too old to be a hippie, Harvey Milk!
I'm not a candidate, I'm part of a movement.  The movement is the candidate.  There's a difference. You don't see the difference. But I do.
Thanks for the swim.  I'm sorry I pissed in your pool.

When the vote came in on election day... out of 32 candidates vying for 6 seats...  We came in 10th. Just a handful short of being the first big eared, gay as a three- dollar-bill, cock sucker ever elected to public office.
So... I went for it again in '75... with just a few... minor... adjustments.
No. Not cute. The shoes are hideous and I hate your hair. You're not fooling anybody.
I'm not gonna let the Pacific Heights biddies write me off again because of a ponytail. I like it.
Cabaret? For two? Lily, is this from one of my kind?
No, no. You won't run into the owner. He died in that suit.
This isn't sexy, Harvey.
Too bad. No more pot, no more bath houses for me and little poo.
We ran and we lost the Supervisors race a second time in '75, but with
It’s a racket. It's 1976 and you still have to be straight, white, Catholic and male to get into the San Francisco police force. It's Racist. Homophobic.
How many people do you consider enemies, Harvey? I've counted five already.

You're right, Art. And many are your closest friends. You see, I don't think State Assembly seats should be awarded on the basis of service to the Democrat Machine. Machines operate on oil and grease. They're dirty, dehumanizing, and too often unresponsive to any needs but those of the operator.
Mr. Milk, I've worked for years as a social worker in this city. I know Sacramento. I know how to get done what we all need done there.

Tell me Mr. Agnos... right here in the Castro, Robert Hillsbourough was murdered for walking home with his long time partner. Stabbed, fifteen times. The last words he heard were "Faggot, Faggot, Faggot..." And you say you’re outraged, but why won’t your liberal establishment return our¡ calls? Why won't they bring these murderers to justice?
You know Harv’, your whole rap's a real downer.
My God, you're handsome up close. I can't tell you how much I'm looking forward to licking you... in the polls.
You talk a lot about what you're against. What are you for? In this town, you gotta give em' a reason for optimism or you're cooked.
Harvey. Dinner.
... get some union boys in the audience, they love me...
I can't control who's in the audience...
Harvey, you need to eat something. Now.
Ladies and Gentlemen. Our apartment is officially off limits.
Downstairs. Time to go. Go. OUT!
Don't say anything.
Sit down and eat.
Scottie--
If you say one thing about politics or your campaign or where you're speaking tonight or whose endorsement you need... I'll stab you with my fork I swear to God.
I just wanted to say...  This is the most delicious meal I've ever eaten in my entire life.
If we lose this one, it'll be just me and you again. I promise.
Phoenix? We should go inside.
You staying up with me?
The election's tomorrow. I won't sleep... Was he good looking?
No. He was hideous.
Hard to tell what's worse. With me, my boyfriends usually find better looking ones to cheat with.
I thought we were gonna spend the rest of our lives together.
Guess what, Cleve Jones.
What?

You’re going to meet the most extraordinary men, the sexiest, brightest, funniest men, and you’re going to fall in love with so many of them, and you won’t know until the end of your life who your greatest friends were or your greatest love was.
Is that supposed to help?
Maybe a little. Or not.

I went to Spain last month... long story. In Barcelona, there was this memorial march for gay people who died under Franco. The police tried to break it up. It started a riot. A bullet ripped through a drag queens scalp, his wig flew off, but he kept fighting. There was blood literally running in the gutter.
We could have a revolution here too. But you can't just use the Castro to cruise. You gotta fight.
You think you'll win?
Winning isn't my strong suit.
Well, I don't "do" losing. Ever. Maybe I should run for office, and you can work for me. If you can do it, I can do it.
Can you assemble a thousand people in an hour?
Yeah, sure.
All right, if I run again, you're my man.
Polls open in 3 hours. Lets hit the bus stops.
And we lost that Assembly Race too, which made me a bona fide three- time, political loser.
We lost, but we lost by less! More votes than ever... I remember when that used to work. I want to show you something.  This is remarkable.

The red is where you did best tonight. If we can get the initiative on district elections to pass, and we can -- we can -- our new Supervisor's District will go right up Market. Right around the Castro.
If you run for Supervisor with this new district, it's a potential landslide. You'll be the first up- front gay man elected to major office in the U.S.
I don't know if I have another one in me. Or Scott.
Gays in Florida can now be removed from their jobs and prosecuted if their "deviant" lifestyles are uncovered.
Bryant, a spokeswoman for the orange juice industry, vowed to bring her campaign to the rest of the country…
Scottie?
I'm sorry sir. I um... I read about you in the paper.
I'm sorry.  I can't talk right--
Sir, I think I want to kill myself.
No. You don't want to do that. Where do you live?
In Minnesota.
You saw me in a paper in Minnesota? How'd I look?
My folks are taking me to this place tomorrow. A hospital. To fix me.
There's nothing wrong with you. Listen to me. You don't have to hurt yourself, okay? Are you there? (only breathing from the other end) Here's what you have to do. Are you listening?
Yes.

Get on a bus. Go to the next biggest city. New York, San Francisco, LA, or even Minneapolis. It doesn't matter. Just leave. You won't be alone. There are people out here just like you who will welcome you and love you. You're not sick. You're not wrong. God does not hate you. Just leave.
I can't.
Yes. You can. Just get on a bus.
I can't... I can't walk, sir. I'm in a wheelchair... Sir.  Are you there?
That's okay. It's going to be okay.
Harvey, you better come down. There's gonna be a riot.
Out of the bar and into the streets! Anita Bryant is coming after you!
If you can't control em', we will.
Give me permission to march them.
Where?
Anywhere.
I know you are angry. I am angry. Follow me through the streets of San Francisco to share our anger!
Are they following in back?
Yes. Where are we going?
I don't know. We have to keep them moving. Exhaust them. March them till they drop. We can’t have a riot tonight.
They're still too hot in back. They'll burn the joint down.
Help me turn them up Van Ness. We'll go all the way to Broadway.
My name is Harvey Milk... And I want to recruit you.

I am here tonight to say, we will no longer sit quietly in our closets. We must fight. Not just in the Castro, or San Francisco, but everywhere the Anitas go. Anita Bryant did not win tonight. Anita Bryant has brought us together... She is going to create a national gay force! And the young people in Richmond, Minnesota, or Jackson, Mississippi, or Woodmere, New York...  Who are coming out and hearing Anita Bryant on television telling them that they're wrong, they're sick, that there is no place for them in this great country, in this world... They're looking to us for something tonight... (his big moment, realizing his true mission) And I say, we have to give them hope!
Hope for a better world... Hope for a better tomorrow... Hope for a better place to come to if the pressures at home are too great...
Hope for the worker who awakens from the American dream to find that all the jobs have left the country...  We gotta give em’ HOPE! When San Francisco changed the voting rules

--they allowed people to elect Supervisors directly from their own neighborhoods.  A black neighborhood could elect an African- American. Chinatown could elect a Chinese Supervisor... And the Castro could choose me or Rick Stokes. But decent art begs for balance, right? So little district 8, two miles south of The Castro--
--promised to stay the same as ever. Conservative, Irish, Catholic and with just the right touch of poetry, they found themselves a handsome ex-cop.

I’m not going to be forced out of San Francisco by splinter groups of social radicals, social deviants, and incorrigibles. You must realize there are thousands upon thousands of frustrated, angry people such as yourselves waiting to unleash a fury that will eradicate the malignancies which blight our beautiful city.
Evelyn Lear's in town, at the Opera house in Tannhauser.
Clearly.
What if she strolls into the Castro for a cocktail? Just one more. We can't let Rick Stokes take it.
Let Rick have it. Nobody thinks you're a fraud here, Harvey. You're not in New York, you're not a closet case asshole anymore. People respect you here. Relax for a year. Get us back on our feet.
If people are ever going to take me seriously, I have to win this one.
Since when did you care if anyone took you seriously?
Anita wants to put us all back in concentration camps. Rick Stokes is too weak to beat her back if she gets to California. I have to be in office to protect us from them.
I can't do another one. I'll be back for my sheets. If you're not here, I’ll see you in the shop next week.
If you leave me right now... I can find someone new.
I know. You're a catch.
--Ring out the old, ring in the new. This is over. Done. I don't want to see anything that say's fucking Assembly. This three-time loser faggot is running for Supervisor goddamn it!
Who the heck is she?
Our new campaign manager. She called last week to volunteer and I asked her to help.
Help or take over? What about Scott?
We need new blood. Guys... Anne Kronenberg. She's a woman... A woman who likes women. Odd, isn't it?
Hi guys. Mr. Milk... Rick Stokes isn't pulling out. He's officially filed to run against you. My friend at The Advocate says David Goodstein is backing him.
A gay candidate against a gay candidate.  It's unfortunate.
They'll split the gay vote. The straights will divide and conquer.
Try to find out when they're going to announce.
You replaced Scott with a lesbian?
Anne worked on a recycling campaign up North. She's very organized.
How do you know she's not a plant from Rick Stokes' campaign?
Are you guys always this paranoid?
Yes. They take after Harvey.
Shouldn't you be doing someone's laundry?
Right after you redecorate my house.
Sir, my girlfriends say you guys don't like women. Just asking, is there a place for us in all this, or are you scared of girls?

Gentleman, we already have a tinker bell, a lotus-blossom, Jim and Dick in your three-piece suits. We need someone to manage things. A woman this time. She's the right price, and she has bigger balls than any of you.
Just give me whatever's left over in the register at the end of the day. So should I call the Chronicle about getting US an endorsement? No?
Young lady, you must be confused. This is my night, not Mr. Milk's.
No, sir. I'm Harvey's new campaign manager. Anne Kronenberg.
Then you should know this race is for District 5, the Castro, not for all of your lesbian... compadres--
I'm sorry. Are we distracting you, Mr. Stokes? I'd hate to screw you up on your big night.
Hi Rick, handsome suit. Annie, do you have any more hand cards?
These people are not from this area. They need to leave. Now.

Not true. Guess where we found them all, Rick? A slew of them. Way up on Valencia Street. Living right there in our district. And you see, as it turns out, Annie here is very, very popular with the ladies.
They've accused me of many things, but never as blatant a lie as this.
First ever newspaper endorsement.
They endorsed you for being a good businessman? Has Scott read this? He'll piss himself.
Yeah? Go show him.
I'm going to The Stud. Meet me out.
Gonna win this time, Milk?
You're more handsome in the posters.
Thank you. Where do you live?
...Palomino's are my favorite horse.  They're smart.  They're fast and compact.  Do you think I'm more like a Palomino or a Stallion?
My father beat me when he found out... So I came here. I stay in this man's house. But I don't like him.
Shh, it's okay, now.  No one's ever going to beat you again.
I love you.
Do you even remember my name? (a beat, Jack shakes his head, he can't remember) Harvey.
I love you, Harvey.
And for the first time in my life, it all came together... the union boys, the women, the seniors, the minorities... All of the us's showed up...
We just want to congratulate him.
I'm sorry. We're all full.
Harvey! Harvey!
It's too crowded.  Sorry!
Harvey!
Scottie--?
The new Mrs. Milk. I give it a week.
Harvey, is this all for you? Are you this famous?
No, darling.  We do this all the time. Do you like it?
Carol Ruth Silver...  Gordon Lau... It's the most liberal Board in years!
What about Dan White?  Did he win?
We'll deal with him tomorrow.
Does this mean, as many straights are concerned, maybe the gays are taking over San Francisco? Are you going to be a Supervisor for all the people?

I have to be. That’s what I was elected for. I have to be there to open up the dialogue for the sensitivities of all people, all their problems, the problems that effect this city effect all of us. Thank you San Francisco! Thank you!

I, Harvey Milk, do solemnly swear that I will support and defend the Constitution of the United States and the Constitution of the State of California against all enemies, foreign and domestic; and that I will well and faithfully discharge the duties upon which I am about to enter and during such time as I hold the office of Supervisor, City and County of San Francisco.
Anita Bryant said gay people brought the drought to California. Looks to me like it’s finally started raining!
This will be the first time in many years that we’ve seen so many new faces on the Board of Supervisors. Do you think such diversity will cripple the Board?
The name of the game on the Board is six votes to get anything passed. So, like it or not, Dan and I are now in bed together… politically speaking.

I've assured Harvey here that my brochure's comments about "social deviants" referred more to junkies than to his people. See, I'm about to have my first child. I have to make sure this city remains a decent place to raise him.
How long were you a fireman, Dan?
A couple of years.
And before that... You were a police officer?
You have something against cops?
No. They tend to have something against me. But firefighters. Now those are true ... They supported me in the last campaign. I support them fully.
I appreciate it.
I meant what I said.  I'd like to work together.
I'll see you at the office, Mr. Milk.
What do you think of my new theater?
A bit over the top.
What's with the suit?
I got it from a friend.

But since that time, people unknown to me, plus my family and friends, have come to me and stated that they want me to stay in office, that they support me...  So I'm asking the Mayor to let me stay in office to serve the people...
He gets dragged into a closed door meeting with the Police Officers Association, and comes out demanding his job back? What do you think they promised him? Or worse, how did they threaten him?
A man has the right to change his mind. Give me some peace.
How many of our propositions has Dan shut down? We could finally settle this police desegregation nonsense. Cement rent control. It's his vote on this Board that stands in our way.
I get it, Harvey. I've been lobbied all week. Enough.
Let me just remind you that you're up for re-election. If you re- appoint Dan, you lose the gay vote. They listen to me. You won't get elected dog catcher.
I'll make my decision on Monday. (as Harvey's on his way out, with a wry grin) You know what you sounded like just now?  Boss Tweed or Mayor Daly.
A "homosexual" with a bit of power... rather scary isn't it, Mr. Mayor?
Yes. This is Dan White.
I'm Barbra Taylor from KCBS. I'm interested in your reaction. I've received information from the Mayor's office that you won't be getting your job back.
I'm sorry. I don't know anything about that.
Hello?
Sorry to wake you up.
What's wrong?
I went to the opera tonight.  Guess who my date was.
Who?
Bidu Sayao herself. She was in the first Puccini I ever saw. The crowd went wild.  It was like I was young again, seeing my first live opera...
If you go to the opera again, you should call me.
Really?
Yes. I'd go with you.
I'd like that.
Harvey?
Look out the window, the sun's coming up.
It's late.  You should get some sleep.
I don't want to miss it.
Miss what?
This.
Hey, wait a minute, Mr.--
My aide was supposed to come down here and let me in the side door, but she never showed up.
And you are?
I'm Dan White. City Supervisor.
Who's it gonna be? Is he replacing Dan with that God Damned liberal lefty Don Horanzy? That'll really shake up the Board, huh?
All I know is that we're getting a new Supervisor today. If Dan shows up, just avoid him.  We don't need a scene today, right Harvey?
Heaven forbid, Dianne.
You can't take this away from me!
The issue is what’s fair, for the people of your district. Take some time off. Spend it with your family.

This time next year we're marching on Washington D.C. It'll look like Market and Castro, but with a million people. Write that down. Tell President Carter we're coming after him next. I'm going to deposit this. I'll be back in 20 minutes.
Say, Harve, can I see you for a minute?
Sure.
Oh no... NO. Last week I got a phone call--

--from Altoona, Pennsylvania, and the voice was quite young, and the person said... "Thanks." You’ve got to elect gay people so that that young child and the thousands upon thousand like him know there’s hope... hope for a better world...
Where is everybody?  Didn't anyone give a damn?
I ask this... If there should be an assassination, I would hope that five, ten, one hundred, a thousand would rise. I would like to see every gay lawyer, every gay architect come out--

If a bullet should enter my brain, let that bullet destroy every closet door... And that's all. I ask for the movement to continue. Because it's not about personal gain, not about ego, not about power... it's about the "us's" out there. Not only gays, but the Blacks, the Asians, the disabled, the seniors, the us’s. Without hope, the us's give up
I know you cannot live on hope alone, but without it, life is not worth living. So you, and you, andyou...
You gotta give em’ hope... you gotta give em' hope.
